title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: LCFS Credit Prices See Uptick Amid Active Trading; RIN Prices Uptrend Continues
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights

(6th October 2025 â€“ 12th October 2025)
  • A total of 1,194,791 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a fall of 6.25% week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed a rise of 54.50% WoW as 5,483,999 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price stood at $53.45/MT for Type 1 transfers, recording a 1.37% rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers saw a rise of 4.31%, priced at $58.81/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price increased by 2.05% WoW; on 14th October, the spot price stood at $55.50/MT, and the benchmark price stood at $56.13/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices rose by 5% WoW. Both D4 and D6 prices rose by 4.21% WoW, while D5 prices rose by 4.26% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

California set a new record for zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) sales in the third quarter of 2025, with 124,755 ZEVs sold, accounting for 29.1% of all new vehicle purchases. The state also became the first in the nation to adopt EV charger reliability and reporting regulations and approved new projects to add and repair charging stations, including more than 1,000 new Level 2 chargers in disadvantaged communities. The California Air Resources Board (CARB) held a Scoping Workshop on October 14, 2025, to discuss the implementation of E15 fuel under Assembly Bill 30 (AB 30). The workshop outlined Californiaâ€™s current fuel and emissions landscape, reviewed the stateâ€™s climate targets for carbon neutrality by 2045, and provided background on AB 30â€™s intent to authorize E15 sales while ensuring environmental and public health protections. cCarbon shared preliminary estimates for Ethanol demand and E15's impact onÂ  LCFS credits by 2027 in an article published last week.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_605853" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Table 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 prices recorded a 1.37% WoW increase, rising from $52.73 to $53.45 this week, and all non-zero transfer prices rose from $56.38 to $58.81, recording an increase of 4.31%. Type 1 trades witnessed 1,194,791 credits exchanged, recording a 6.25% fall WoW. The total transaction volumes for all non-zero trades saw an increase of 54.50% WoW, reaching 5,483,999 credits.

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_605854" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices rose by 2.05% WoW, reaching $55.26/MT. On 14th October, the spot price stood at $55.50/MT. The benchmark price, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $56.13/MT as of 14th October.

LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_605855" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, 181 trades were reported in the market, with 38 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $100 for two Type 2 transactions at a volume of 32,788 and 15,078 credits. The lowest transfer by price was $43.00 per credit for a Type 1 transaction reported for 2000 credits. The largest volume of transfers involved 500,000 credits for two Type 2 transactions priced at $51.50 and $77.10. The smallest volume of transfer involved 48 credits for a Type 1 transaction priced at $49.02.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_605856" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average spot Type 1 price stood at $53.17 in October so far, falling from $53.94 in September. Trade volumes for the month of October stood at 8.30 M as of 14th October, 2025.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 6th â€“ 12th October 2025, total VPT rose by 28.34% WoW, with rise in all non-zero credit prices for the last week. [caption id="attachment_605857" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption]

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN Weekly Price Trend

Â RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices rose by 5.00% WoW. Both D4 and D6 prices rose by 4.21% WoW, while D5 price rose by 4.26% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming DatesÂ 

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments This Week

  • Growth Energy's Letter to California Senator Catherine Blakespear Urging Support for AB 30: Growth Energy, representing 97 U.S. biorefineries that produce over nine billion gallons of renewable fuel annually, supports Californiaâ€™s AB 30 (Alvarez), which would legalize the sale and use of E15. The organization emphasizes that E15 offers economic and environmental benefits, potentially saving California drivers up to $0.20 per gallon and $2.7 billion annually if E15 replaces E10.
  • Scoping Workshop on E15 Use in California: CARB conducted a public workshop on October 14, to discuss introducing gasoline containing up to 15% ethanol (E15) in California. At this workshop, CARB staff provided an overview of the scientific evaluation of E15, current regulatory requirements, and concepts for potential regulatory updates as well as additional topics regarding the use of E15 in California.
  • California Smashes ZEV Sales Record in Q3: Governor Gavin Newsom announced that Californians purchased 124,755 zero-emission vehicles (ZEVs) in the third quarter of 2025, representing 29.1% of new car sales, the highest quarterly sales share ever reported in California. Despite the numerous setbacks to the ZEV industry brought about by the federal administration, Californians made it clear they want to drive ZEVs. The ZEV market is also diversifying in California, with 146 ZEV models available in Q1, up from 105 models in Q1 of last year. Of the 124,755 ZEVs purchased in Q3, 108,685 of those were EVs.
  • Governor Newsom Vetoes Senate Bill 298 on Maritime Emissions Reduction: Governor Gavin Newsom has vetoed Senate Bill 298, citing concerns over its potential unintended consequences and the need for further refinement. In his veto message, Newsom highlighted ambiguities in the billâ€™s language and possible overlaps with existing legislation that could lead to confusion or conflicting interpretations. He emphasized the importance of ensuring that new laws do not disrupt established policies or create unnecessary complexities in implementation.
